title: Versico Roofing Systems in Worcester, MA | Commercial Roofers of Worcester
metaDescription: Versico VersiGard TPO and EPDM for Worcester commercial reroofs: budget-priced single-ply membrane with fastening rated for local snow and wind loads.

html:
Versico's Value-Tier TPO and EPDM for Worcester Reroofs

Versico manufactures a straightforward TPO and EPDM line under the VersiGard name, and it's the manufacturer we reach for most often on budget-driven Worcester reroofs where an owner wants a solid single-ply system without paying for accessory depth or brand positioning the job doesn't need.

VersiGard TPO and EPDM Cover the Standard Cases

Versico's VersiGard TPO comes in the same 60- and 80-mil range we spec across other manufacturers, along with fleece-backed options for recover work, and VersiGard EPDM covers standard black membrane for the recover and budget-driven jobs where EPDM still makes sense on Worcester's older building stock. The product line doesn't try to be everything; it covers the two most commonly specced membrane chemistries well and at a competitive price.

We stock VersiGard TPO and EPDM in standard roll widths for most Worcester jobs, and lead times generally track close to the other manufacturers on our regular list. Versico doesn't manufacture PVC, so a chemical-exposure roof needing that specific resistance goes to Carlisle, Elevate, or Sarnafil instead, and we say so plainly rather than substituting a Versico membrane that isn't rated for the exposure.

Where Versico Wins on Price

On a straightforward mechanically attached reroof without unusual chemical exposure or complex penetration counts, Versico's pricing regularly comes in at or below Carlisle, GAF, and Elevate on the bids we run side by side. For an owner managing several smaller commercial buildings across our Worcester service area on a tight capital budget, that price positioning makes Versico a frequent recommendation, not a fallback choice.

We still run the same performance comparison on wind uplift, seam chemistry, and warranty terms before recommending Versico over a more established name, since a lower price only matters if the membrane meets the same performance bar the job requires.

Cold-Weather Welding and Seam Performance

VersiGard TPO welds with standard hot-air equipment and follows published temperature guidance similar to the other TPO manufacturers on this list. We adjust welder settings for Worcester's cold-weather installation windows the same way we would on any TPO job, and we haven't seen a meaningful difference in seam reliability between Versico and the higher-priced manufacturers when installation procedure is followed correctly.

VersiGard EPDM's seam tape system follows comparable cold-weather adhesion rules to Carlisle's Sure-Seal or Elevate's RubberGard tape, and we apply the same close seam inspection during install regardless of which manufacturer's tape is on the roof. Versico publishes its own minimum-temperature guidance for that tape system, and we follow it on every cold-weather EPDM job the same way we follow every other manufacturer's cold-weather rules.

  • Budget-driven reroofs on smaller commercial buildings
  • Owners managing multiple properties on a tight capital plan
  • Standard mechanically attached TPO installs without unusual exposure
  • Recover jobs specced in VersiGard EPDM over sound existing substrates
  • Bids where competitive pricing outweighs manufacturer brand positioning

Insulation and Accessory Availability

Versico also manufactures polyiso insulation board and a standard accessory line for VersiGard membrane, sized to meet the Massachusetts stretch code's insulation minimum when specced at the correct thickness for the building's code cycle. The accessory catalog is narrower than Sarnafil's PVC-focused line or Carlisle's broader offering, which is consistent with Versico's positioning as a value manufacturer rather than a full-service accessory house.

We calculate the specific board thickness a given building needs against the code cycle Worcester has adopted rather than assuming a standard Versico board thickness applies everywhere. On a job where a more complex accessory need comes up mid-project, we'll sometimes mix a Carlisle or JM accessory into an otherwise Versico-specced roof rather than forcing a narrower catalog to cover a detail it wasn't built for.

Warranty Terms Against the Rest of the Field

Versico's warranty terms track similarly to other manufacturers, tied to the specific assembly spec and our certification status at time of install. Coverage length on a comparable assembly runs close to what Carlisle or GAF would offer at a similar price point, and we verify current certification status with Versico before quoting any warranty number, the same as with every manufacturer on our list.

We document the specific assembly and certification status behind every Versico warranty number the same way we do for Carlisle or GAF, since an owner comparing manufacturers on price alone should still see the coverage terms side by side before deciding. A lower installed cost doesn't automatically mean a shorter warranty, and on many standard assemblies it doesn't.

Questions About Versico's Roofing Line

Is Versico a lower-quality membrane because it costs less?

Not based on core performance. VersiGard TPO and EPDM meet the same industry testing standards as other major manufacturers; the lower price reflects a narrower accessory catalog and brand positioning, not weaker membrane chemistry.

Does Versico offer fleece-backed TPO for recover jobs?

Yes, VersiGard TPO includes a fleece-backed option for recover applications over sound existing substrates.

How does Versico's pricing compare to Carlisle or GAF?

It typically comes in at or below those manufacturers on standard mechanically attached reroofs, which is the main reason we recommend it on budget-driven jobs.

Can Versico's insulation meet the Massachusetts stretch code?

Their polyiso board comes in thicknesses that can meet stretch code minimums; we calculate the required thickness for each specific building.

What warranty term can I expect on a Versico system?

Coverage length depends on the assembly spec and our certification status with Versico at time of install, comparable to what other manufacturers in a similar price range offer.
